summary: The ideal management of a woman with bilateral cornual block identified on hysterosalpingography involves avoiding invasive procedures due to lack of effectiveness; instead, intracytoplasmic sperm injection (ICSI) combined with in vitro fertilization (IVF) is recommended because it circumvents the anatomical defect without relying on normal tubal transport. This approach has been repeatedly confirmed in medical guidelines as superior over all other treatment modalities when dealing with complete tubal occlusions involving the cornua.
